entities.name: User
entities.description: System user account for authentication and access control. Represents owners, waiters/cashiers, and chefs with configurable permissions.

business_rules.rule: Each user must have a unique username
After login, ALL users are routed to the Main Dashboard Hub
Owner can create, edit, and deactivate user accounts
Failed login attempts are logged
Owner can set and reset PIN per user for shift operations

entities.name: UserPermission
entities.description: Per-user permission override for module access. Owner configures these via checklist UI to grant or revoke specific module access beyond role defaults.

business_rules.rule: Only Owner can access the access control panel
Permission changes take effect immediately
Each role has default permissions that can be overridden per individual user
Core permissions cannot be removed

entities.name: MenuItem
entities.description: A dish or drink on the restaurant menu with pricing, category, availability, and optional image. Linked to recipes for cooking instructions and inventory for stock deduction.

business_rules.rule: Unavailable items are hidden from the order screen
Price changes take effect immediately for new orders
Each menu item can be linked to a recipe and ingredient list

entities.name: MenuCategory
entities.description: Category grouping for menu items (e.g., food, drink, dessert, snack). Used for filtering and organization during order creation.

business_rules.rule: Owner can create, edit, and delete categories

entities.name: Recipe
entities.description: Cooking guide for a menu item containing step-by-step instructions, required ingredients with quantities, cooking time, and difficulty level. Accessible from Kitchen Display.

business_rules.rule: Recipe ingredients must reference inventory items for stock deduction
Only Owner or authorized chef can edit recipes
Recipe is viewable from Kitchen Display per order item

entities.name: RecipeIngredient
entities.description: An ingredient entry within a recipe, linking a recipe to an inventory item with the required quantity and unit. Used for automatic stock deduction when orders are placed.

business_rules.rule: Recipe ingredients must reference existing inventory items for stock deduction linkage

entities.name: RecipeStep
entities.description: An ordered step within a recipe's cooking instructions. Each step contains the instruction text and its sequence number.

entities.name: InventoryItem
entities.description: An ingredient or supply tracked in inventory with current stock quantity, unit of measure, and minimum threshold for low-stock alerts.

business_rules.rule: Stock deduction is triggered automatically when an order is confirmed
steps: For each order item, look up recipe
For each recipe ingredient, deduct quantityRequired * orderItem.quantity from inventory
Update stockStatus based on new quantity vs minimumThreshold

business_rules.rule: Deduction amounts are based on recipe ingredient definitions
Stock cannot go below zero - system warns if insufficient
Only authorized users can manually adjust stock (restock or correction)

entities.name: StockMovement
entities.description: Audit log entry for every inventory stock change - restock, auto-deduction from orders, and manual adjustments. Provides traceability for stock levels.

business_rules.rule: Stock movement is logged for every inventory change (auto-deduction, restock, adjustment)

entities.name: Table
entities.description: A restaurant table with seating capacity and real-time occupancy status. Tables are linked to active orders.

business_rules.rule: Table status updates automatically when order is created/paid
Owner can configure total number of tables

entities.name: Order
entities.description: A customer order linked to a table, containing line items from the menu. Tracks the full lifecycle from creation through kitchen processing to payment. Supports additional items appended to existing orders.

business_rules.rule: Order must have at least one menu item
Each order is linked to a specific table
If a table already has an active (unpaid) order, cashier can ADD items to that existing order instead of creating a new one
Additional items are appended to the same order with Tambahan label
Order total recalculated automatically after adding items
Order can be cancelled before payment (status -> cancelled)
Discount applied before PPN: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) * (1 + PPN rate)

entities.name: OrderItem
entities.description: A line item within an order, linking to a menu item with quantity, unit price at time of order, and optional special notes. Tracks whether the item was added as a tambahan (additional).

business_rules.rule: Line total is calculated as unitPrice * quantity
Additional items from same table appear as new items in the SAME order card with Tambahan badge

entities.name: Transaction
entities.description: A completed payment record storing the full financial details including subtotal, PPN breakdown, discount, payment method, and receipt information. Linked to shift for reconciliation.

business_rules.rule: Payment cannot be processed if amount paid is less than grand total
Transaction stores SPECIFIC payment method name (e.g., GoPay, OVO, Transfer BCA) - NOT generic Digital
PPN amount = (subtotal - discount) * (ppnRate / 100)
Grand total = (subtotal - discount) + ppnAmount
For digital payments: no change calculation needed (exact amount)
Transaction is recorded in sales history with full PPN info

entities.name: Shift
entities.description: A cashier shift with opening/closing lifecycle. Tracks opening balance, all cash and digital transactions during the shift, and requires cash reconciliation at closing with mandatory notes for discrepancies.

business_rules.rule: Cashier MUST verify PIN before opening a shift
Cashier MUST open a shift before processing any payment
Only one active shift per cashier at a time
Expected cash = opening_balance + cash_sales - cash_refunds_from_voids
Closing is a ONE-WAY session - cashier cannot return to operations after initiating close
If discrepancy exists (not match), notes are MANDATORY - cashier must explain before closing
Closed shift data is immutable and logged for audit

entities.name: VoidRecord
entities.description: An immutable audit record of a voided or cancelled order/transaction. Requires PIN verification and mandatory reason. Affects shift cash reconciliation.

business_rules.rule: PIN verification required before any void operation
Cancel before payment: order is cancelled, items returned to available, no cash impact
Void after payment (full): full refund tracked, expected cash reduced in shift
Void reason is MANDATORY - kasir must select reason
All voids are logged immutably for audit trail
Expected cash formula updated: opening + cash_sales - cash_refunds_from_voids

entities.name: Discount
entities.description: A discount or promo rule created by the owner. Can be percentage-based or fixed amount. Applied to whole orders or specific items. Active discounts appear as banners during order creation.

business_rules.rule: Owner creates and manages discount/promo options in settings
Discount applied BEFORE PPN calculation: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) + PPN
Only one discount per order (prevent stacking)
Owner can activate/deactivate discounts anytime

entities.name: KitchenNotification
entities.description: A transient notification created when chef marks an order as ready. Displayed on the waiter POS screen until confirmed as served. Ephemeral - deleted after confirmation.

business_rules.rule: When chef marks order as ready, notification is pushed to waiter/cashier POS screen immediately
Waiter taps notification to confirm served - notification is removed

entities.name: PosSettings
entities.description: Singleton configuration for the POS system including PPN/tax settings, restaurant info for receipts, and receipt customization. Only one record exists system-wide.

business_rules.rule: PPN rate is configurable by owner in system settings
PPN applies to all transactions when enabled
Changes to PPN rate apply to new transactions only, not retroactively

entities.name: EwalletProvider
entities.description: Configuration for an e-wallet payment provider (GoPay, OVO, Dana, ShopeePay). Each provider can be enabled/disabled and has its own QR code image for display during payment.

business_rules.rule: E-Wallet provider shows specific QR code for that provider during payment

recommendations.reason: Comprehensive audit logging for all system actions (login, order changes, void operations, settings changes) would strengthen accountability. Currently, individual entities log their own audit trails (VoidRecord, StockMovement), but a centralized audit log would provide holistic tracking.

F-009 business rule states 'Failed login attempts are logged'. A dedicated LoginAttempt entity would capture IP, timestamp, username, success/failure for security monitoring. Currently this is noted as a business rule but has no dedicated storage.
Adding soft-delete (isDeleted, deletedAt) to key entities (User, MenuItem, InventoryItem) would preserve referential integrity for historical records while hiding deleted items from active views.

decisions_requiring_approval.item: Stock deduction timing: When should inventory be auto-deducted?
options: A: At order confirmation (when waiter submits order) - F-004 says 'when order is confirmed'
B: At payment completion (when transaction is recorded) - UF-001 step 11 says 'stock auto-deducted' at payment
C: At order submission but with reversal on cancellation/void
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Option C (deduct at submission, reverse on cancel/void) provides the best real-time stock accuracy while maintaining data integrity. This matches F-004's 'when order is confirmed' timing and handles the UF-010 void case by creating a reversal StockMovement. The timing_conflicts in cross_domain_flows explicitly flag this ambiguity between F-004 and UF-001 step 11.

decisions_requiring_approval.item: PIN verification scope: Should PIN hashes be stored on the User entity or in a separate PinVerification entity?
options: A: Store pinHash as a User attribute (simpler, current design)
B: Create a separate PinVerification entity with context-specific PINs (shift, void, POS entry)
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: The prototype uses a single PIN per user for all verification contexts (shift opening, void operations, POS entry). A single pinHash on User is simpler and matches the BA spec which says 'Owner can set and reset PIN per user'. The three PIN contexts (S-014 shift, POS gate, void) all use the same PIN - they are different UI gates, not different PINs.

decisions_requiring_approval.item: Order-level vs item-level cooking status tracking
options: A: Track cooking status at ORDER level only (simpler, current Order.orderStatus)
B: Track cooking status at both ORDER and ITEM level (allows partial ready states)
C: Track only at ITEM level, derive order status from item statuses
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: The current design includes both Order.orderStatus and OrderItem.cookingStatus. This allows the chef to mark individual items as ready while the order-level status reflects the overall state. The Kitchen Display shows per-item status while the Order List shows per-order status. This dual tracking is needed for the 'additional items' feature where new items may be pending while original items are already served.

decisions_requiring_approval.item: Transaction entity redundancy: Should transaction store denormalized order data or reference order via FK only?
options: A: Denormalize key fields (tableNumber, subtotal, ppn, discount) into Transaction for immutable receipt data
B: Reference Order via FK only, compute all values from Order + OrderItems at read time
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Transaction records must be immutable for financial auditing. If the order or menu item prices change after payment, the transaction should still reflect the original values. Denormalizing subtotal, ppn, discount, and payment details into Transaction ensures receipt reprinting always shows the correct historical values. This also matches the prototype's data model where transactions store full financial snapshots.
